---
title: "ggilustrate"
author: "Pham Minh Hieu"
date: "2025-05-22"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

---
```{r}

```

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(reticulate)
library(dotenv)

# Load .env file
dotenv::load_dot_env(file = ".env")
api_key <- Sys.getenv("REPLICATE_API_TOKEN")

# Ensure reticulate uses the correct Python environment
use_virtualenv("./venv", required = TRUE)
py_config()

# Import Python utility functions
script_dir <- getwd() 
utils <- import_from_path("utils", path = script_dir)

# Define file paths
plot_path <- "my_plot.png"
sketch_path <- "my_sketch.png"

# Define the prompt for the AI illustration
illustration_prompt <- "A vibrant, artistic representation of car efficiency trends, perhaps using stylized vehicles or abstract energy flows."

# --- 1. Create and Save ggplot --- 
print("Creating ggplot...")
data(mtcars) # Use the built-in mtcars dataset

p <- ggplot(mtcars, aes(x = wt, y = mpg, color = factor(cyl))) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  labs(title = "Car Weight vs. MPG by Cylinders",
       x = "Weight (1000 lbs)",
       y = "Miles per Gallon (MPG)",
       color = "Cylinders") +
  theme_minimal()

print(paste("Saving plot to:", plot_path))
ggsave(plot_path, plot = p, width = 6, height = 4, dpi = 150)

# --- 2. Generate Sketch using Python/OpenCV --- 
print("Generating sketch from plot...")
sketch_file <- utils$detect_edges(plot_path, sketch_path)

if (!is.null(sketch_file)) {
  print(paste("Sketch generated:", sketch_file))
  
  # --- 3. Generate Illustration using Python/Replicate --- 
  print("Generating AI illustration from sketch...")
  illustration_url <- utils$generate_illustration(sketch_file, illustration_prompt)
  
  if (!is.null(illustration_url)) {
    print(paste("Illustration generated! URL:", illustration_url))
    # You can open the URL in a browser or download the image
    # browseURL(illustration_url) 
  } else {
    print("Failed to generate illustration.")
  }
} else {
  print("Failed to generate sketch.")
}

print("Demo script finished.")


```
